report_name: Microsoft's Massive April Patch Tuesday Fixes Actively Exploited SharePoint Zero-Day and 164 Other Flaws

attack_patterns.name: Exploit Public-Facing Application
attack_patterns.description: The actively exploited SharePoint vulnerability (CVE-2026-32201) allows an unauthenticated attacker to exploit the public-facing application to view and modify sensitive information.

attack_patterns.name: Command and Scripting Interpreter
attack_patterns.description: Malicious scripts, such as JavaScript in a cross-site scripting (XSS) attack, can be executed in the context of a victim's browser by exploiting the SharePoint spoofing vulnerability (CVE-2026-32201).

attack_patterns.name: Exploitation for Privilege Escalation
attack_patterns.description: The publicly disclosed vulnerability in Microsoft Defender (CVE-2026-33825) can be exploited to gain higher privileges on a compromised system.

attack_patterns.name: Exploitation of Remote Services
attack_patterns.description: Vulnerabilities in the Windows TCP/IP stack (CVE-2026-33827) and IKE service (CVE-2026-33824) allow for remote code execution by exploiting network services.

tools.name: BlueHammer
tools.description: A proof-of-concept exploit for the Microsoft Defender elevation of privilege vulnerability (CVE-2026-33825) that was published on GitHub.

vulnerabilities.name: CVE-2026-32201
vulnerabilities.description: An actively exploited spoofing vulnerability in Microsoft SharePoint Server that allows an unauthenticated attacker to view and modify sensitive information, potentially via cross-site scripting (XSS).
vulnerabilities.kev_required_action: Apply mitigations per vendor instructions, follow applicable BOD 22-01 guidance for cloud services, or discontinue use of the product if mitigations are unavailable.
vulnerabilities.cisa_vuln_name: Microsoft SharePoint Server Improper Input Validation Vulnerability

vulnerabilities.name: CVE-2026-33827
vulnerabilities.description: A critical, potentially 'wormable' remote code execution vulnerability in the Windows TCP/IP stack that can be exploited by an unauthenticated attacker on systems with IPv6 and IPSec enabled.

vulnerabilities.name: CVE-2026-33824
vulnerabilities.description: A critical remote code execution vulnerability in the Windows Internet Key Exchange (IKE) service, allowing an unauthenticated attacker to execute code on an affected VPN server.
affected_products: Windows Internet Key Exchange (IKE) Service Extensions

vulnerabilities.name: CVE-2026-33825
vulnerabilities.description: A publicly disclosed elevation of privilege vulnerability in Microsoft Defender with a proof-of-concept exploit named 'BlueHammer' available.

indicators.name: Potential SharePoint XSS Activity (CVE-2026-32201)
indicators.pattern:
title: Potential SharePoint XSS Activity (CVE-2026-32201)
id: 8f2c1b3a-9e4d-4f8c-a1b2-3d4e5f6a7b8c
status: experimental
description: Detects potential Cross-Site Scripting (XSS) attempts against Microsoft SharePoint Server, possibly related to CVE-2026-32201. This rule looks for suspicious script-like syntax in URLs targeting common SharePoint paths.
references:
  - https://securityaffairs.com/161986/security/microsoft-patch-tuesday-april-2026.html
author: cyber.netsecops.io
date: 2026-04-16
tags:
  - attack.t1190
  - attack.initial_access
falsepositives:
  - Legitimate but unusual SharePoint customizations.
  - Web vulnerability scanners.
level: medium
logsource:
  category: proxy
detection:
  selection_path:
    c-uri|contains:
      - '/_layouts/'
      - '/pages/'
  selection_payload:
    c-uri|contains:
      - '<script'
      - 'onerror='
      - 'onload='
      - 'javascript:'
  condition: all of selection_*
indicators.description: Detects web requests to SharePoint containing URL patterns and script tags indicative of XSS attempts related to CVE-2026-32201.

indicators.name: Suspicious Child Process of Microsoft Defender (CVE-2026-33825)
indicators.pattern:
title: Suspicious Child Process of Microsoft Defender (CVE-2026-33825)
id: 1a2b3c4d-5e6f-7a8b-9c0d-1e2f3a4b5c6d
status: experimental
description: Detects suspicious child processes spawned by the Microsoft Defender service (MsMpEng.exe or MpOAV.exe), which could indicate exploitation of an elevation of privilege vulnerability like CVE-2026-33825 ('BlueHammer').
references:
  - https://securityaffairs.com/161986/security/microsoft-patch-tuesday-april-2026.html
author: cyber.netsecops.io
date: 2026-04-16
tags:
  - attack.t1068
  - attack.privilege_escalation
falsepositives:
  - Legitimate administrative scripts or diagnostic tools interacting with Defender.
level: high
logsource:
  category: process_creation
  product: windows
detection:
  selection_parent:
    ParentImage|endswith:
      - '\MsMpEng.exe'
      - '\MpOAV.exe'
  selection_child:
    Image|endswith:
      - '\powershell.exe'
      - '\cmd.exe'
      - '\wscript.exe'
      - '\cscript.exe'
      - '\rundll32.exe'
  condition: all of selection_*
indicators.description: Detects Microsoft Defender processes spawning suspicious child processes like powershell.exe, which could indicate exploitation of CVE-2026-33825.

indicators.name: Anomalous IKE Network Traffic
indicators.pattern:
title: Anomalous IKE Network Traffic
id: 7d6c5b4a-3e2d-1f0a-9b8c-7a6b5c4d3e2f
status: experimental
description: Detects network traffic on UDP ports 500 and 4500, used by the Internet Key Exchange (IKE) protocol. High volume or unusual patterns could indicate scanning or exploitation attempts against vulnerabilities like CVE-2026-33824.
references:
  - https://securityaffairs.com/161986/security/microsoft-patch-tuesday-april-2026.html
author: cyber.netsecops.io
date: 2026-04-16
tags:
  - attack.t1210
  - attack.lateral_movement
falsepositives:
  - Legitimate VPN connections. This rule is likely to be noisy and requires tuning and correlation.
level: low
logsource:
  category: network_connection
detection:
  selection:
    DestinationPort:
      - 500
      - 4500
    Protocol: 'udp'
  condition: selection
indicators.description: Detects network traffic on UDP ports 500/4500, which could indicate scanning or exploitation attempts against the IKE service (CVE-2026-33824).

relationships.source_name: Suspicious Child Process of Microsoft Defender (CVE-2026-33825)
